Package org.apache.juneau.rest.client

Class RestRequest

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.client.RestRequest
public final class RestRequest extends Object
Fluent builder for a single HTTP request.

Obtain instances from the RestClient factory methods (e.g. RestClient.get(String)).

Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*). It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the next major Juneau release (and possibly earlier).

Since:
9.2.1

  • Method Details

    • headers

      public RestRequest headers(HttpHeader... value)
      Adds request headers.
      Parameters:
      value - The headers to add. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • header

      public RestRequest header(String name, String value)
      Adds a request header.
      Parameters:
      name - The header name. Must not be null.
      value - The header value. May be null to omit.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • header

      public RestRequest header(String name, Supplier<String> value)
      Adds a request header with a lazy value.
      Parameters:
      name - The header name. Must not be null.
      value - Supplier for the value. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • hasHeader

      public boolean hasHeader(String name)
      Returns true if a header with the given name (case-insensitive) is already set on this request.

      Used by the next-gen remote-proxy engine to honor the precedence of a caller-supplied @Header parameter over an annotation-declared accept/contentType attribute.

      Parameters:
      name - The header name. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      true if the header is present.

    • queryData

      public RestRequest queryData(HttpPart... value)
      Adds query parameters.
      Parameters:
      value - The parts to add. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • queryData

      public RestRequest queryData(String name, String value)
      Adds a query parameter.
      Parameters:
      name - The parameter name. Must not be null.
      value - The parameter value. May be null to omit.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • queryData

      public RestRequest queryData(String name, Supplier<String> value)
      Adds a query parameter with a lazy value.
      Parameters:
      name - The parameter name. Must not be null.
      value - Supplier for the value. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • formData

      public RestRequest formData(HttpPart... value)
      Adds form fields (for application/x-www-form-urlencoded bodies).
      Parameters:
      value - The parts to add. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • formData

      public RestRequest formData(String name, String value)
      Adds a form field.
      Parameters:
      name - The field name. Must not be null.
      value - The field value. May be null to omit.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • pathData

      public RestRequest pathData(HttpPart... value)
      Adds path variable substitutions for URI templates (e.g. replaces {id} in the URL).
      Parameters:
      value - The path parts to apply. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • pathData

      public RestRequest pathData(String name, Object value)
      Adds a path variable.
      Parameters:
      name - The variable name (without braces). Must not be null.
      value - The replacement value. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • body

      public RestRequest body(HttpBody value)
      Sets the request body directly from an HttpBody.
      Parameters:
      value - The body. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • body

      public RestRequest body(Object value) throws IOException
      Sets the request body from an arbitrary Java object.

      The object is first run through the client's body converter chain. The default converters handle: HttpBody (passthrough), InputStream, byte[], and File. Custom converters can be registered on the builder.

      If no converter matches, the object is serialized with the client's explicitly-configured default serializer (RestClient.getDefaultSerializer()) and sent as a string body using the serializer's content type. When no default serializer is configured, an IllegalStateException is raised (there is no implicit JSON fallback) — configure one via RestClient.Builder.defaultSerializer(org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer).

      Parameters:
      value - The body object. May be null to clear the body.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a converter fails or the default serializer fails.
      IllegalStateException - If no converter matches and no default serializer is configured on the client.
      IllegalArgumentException - If the default serializer produces output that is neither text nor byte[]. Binary (byte[]) serializer output is sent as a binary body using the serializer's media type (falling back to application/octet-stream when the media type is null).

    • streamBodyEntity

      public RestRequest streamBodyEntity(RecordStreamBody value)
      Sets the request body to a token/record-streaming cursor body.

      Convenience for body(streamBody) that documents streaming intent and avoids a cast. The RecordStreamBody writes directly to the transport output stream during run(), so large payloads are streamed to the wire without being buffered in memory.

      Example:

      client.post("/bulk-upload") .streamBodyEntity(RecordStreamBody.records(w -> { for (Bean b : source()) w.write(b); })) .run();

      Parameters:
      value - The streaming body. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • bodyString

      public RestRequest bodyString(String value)
      Sets the request body to a raw string (UTF-8, text/plain).

      Unlike body(Object), this bypasses the converter chain and sends the string as-is.

      Parameters:
      value - The string body. May be null to clear the body.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • debug

      public RestRequest debug()
      Flags this request for verbose debug logging.

      When set, the configured RestLogger will receive an entry with RestLogEntry.isDebug() true, enabling full request/response header and body logging for this call only.

      Returns:
      This object.

    • timeout

      public RestRequest timeout(Duration value)
      Sets a per-call response timeout for this request.

      Threaded onto the TransportRequest and applied by the transport as the response/read timeout. Connect timeouts remain a client-level setting.

      Parameters:
      value - The response timeout. May be null to use the transport default.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • interceptors

      public RestRequest interceptors(RestCallInterceptor... value)
      Adds per-request lifecycle interceptors.

      These are unioned with the client-level interceptors when the request runs: the client-level (builder) interceptors fire first, then these in the order added. The next-gen remote-proxy engine uses this to layer annotation-declared interface-level then method-level interceptors after the builder-configured ones.

      Parameters:
      value - The interceptors to add. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • isBodyRepeatable

      public boolean isBodyRepeatable()
      Returns true if this request's body can be safely re-sent (retry gating).

      A request with no body is trivially repeatable. Otherwise the answer is delegated to the underlying body's HttpBody.isRepeatable() (or TransportBody.isRepeatable() for a pre-converted body): buffered bodies (string/byte[]/file/serialized POJO) are repeatable; one-shot streaming bodies (InputStream/Reader/piped) are not.

      Returns:
      true if the body is absent or repeatable.

    • getMethod

      public String getMethod()
      Returns the HTTP method for this request (e.g. "GET").
      Returns:
      The method. Never null.

    • getUri

      public URI getUri()
      Returns the fully resolved URI for this request, or null if run() has not been called yet.
      Returns:
      The URI, possibly null.

    • run

      public RestResponse run() throws TransportException, RestCallException
      Executes the request and returns the response.

      The caller is responsible for closing the returned RestResponse.

      Returns:
      The response. Never null.
      Throws:
      TransportException - If a network-level error occurs.
      RestCallException - If the response could not be processed.
